validate_order = Task(id='validate_order', name='Validate Order', task_type='service')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(receive_order)
process.add_element(validate_order)
process.add_element(process_payment)
process.add_element(ship_order)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(validate_order, process_payment)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_payment, ship_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(ship_order, end)

parts.text: I will create an order processing workflow with the following steps: start event, "receive order" user task, "validate order" service task, "process payment" service task, "ship order" user task, and an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process and ensure all elements are correctly imported and connected. Finally, I will use the `validate_process` tool to check the validity of the generated BPMN code.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='order_processing_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
receive_order = UserTask(id='receive_order', name='Receive Order')
validate_order = Task(id='validate_order', name='Validate Order', task_type='service')
process_payment = Task(id='process_payment', name='Process Payment', task_type='service')
ship_order = UserTask(id='ship_order', name='Ship Order')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_order)
process.add_element(validate_order)
process.add_element(process_payment)
process.add_element(ship_order)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_order, validate_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(validate_order, process_payment)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_payment, ship_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(ship_order, end)

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, Task, EndEvent, SendTask, ManualTask

process = Process(name='document_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
scan_document = UserTask(id='scan_document', name='Scan Document')
extract_data = Task(id='extract_data', name='Extract Data', task_type='service')
send_notification = SendTask(id='send_notification', name='Send Notification')
manual_filing = ManualTask(id='manual_filing', name='Manual Filing')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(scan_document)
process.add_element(extract_data)
process.add_element(send_notification)
process.add_element(manual_filing)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, scan_document)
process.add_sequence_flow(scan_document, extract_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(extract_data, send_notification)
process.add_sequence_flow(send_notification, manual_filing)
process.add_sequence_flow(manual_filing, end)

results.generated_code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ExclusiveGateway, ParallelGateway, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='loan_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
submit_application = UserTask(id='submit_application', name='Submit Application')

credit_check_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='credit_check_gateway', name='Check Credit', gateway_type='exclusive')

process_docs = Task(id='process_docs', name='Process Docs', task_type='service')
verify_income = Task(id='verify_income', name='Verify Income', task_type='service')
parallel_gateway = ParallelGateway(id='parallel_gateway', name='Parallel Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')
join_gateway = ParallelGateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')

approve = Task(id='approve', name='Approve', task_type='user')
reject = Task(id='reject', name='Reject', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(submit_application)
process.add_element(credit_check_gateway)
process.add_element(process_docs)
process.add_element(verify_income)
process.add_element(parallel_gateway)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(approve)
process.add_element(reject)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, submit_application)
process.add_sequence_flow(submit_application, credit_check_gateway)

# Good credit path
process.add_sequence_flow(credit_check_gateway, process_docs, condition='credit_good')
process.add_sequence_flow(credit_check_gateway, reject, condition='credit_bad')

process.add_sequence_flow(process_docs, parallel_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(verify_income, parallel_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_gateway, join_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, approve)
process.add_sequence_flow(approve, end)

# Bad credit path
process.add_sequence_flow(reject, end)

parts.text: I will create a loan process with a start event, a "submit application" user task, an exclusive gateway to check credit. If the credit is good, it will lead to a parallel gateway where "process docs" and "verify income" tasks run concurrently. These will then join before leading to an "approve" task and the end event. If the credit is bad, it will lead to a "reject" task and then to the end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process and use `Task` with appropriate `task_type` for user and service tasks. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ExclusiveGateway, ParallelGateway, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='loan_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
submit_application = UserTask(id='submit_application', name='Submit Application')

credit_check_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='credit_check_gateway', name='Check Credit', gateway_type='exclusive')

process_docs = Task(id='process_docs', name='Process Docs', task_type='service')
verify_income = Task(id='verify_income', name='Verify Income', task_type='service')
parallel_gateway = ParallelGateway(id='parallel_gateway', name='Parallel Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')
join_gateway = ParallelGateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')

approve = Task(id='approve', name='Approve', task_type='user')
reject = Task(id='reject', name='Reject', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(submit_application)
process.add_element(credit_check_gateway)
process.add_element(process_docs)
process.add_element(verify_income)
process.add_element(parallel_gateway)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(approve)
process.add_element(reject)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, submit_application)
process.add_sequence_flow(submit_application, credit_check_gateway)

# Good credit path
process.add_sequence_flow(credit_check_gateway, process_docs, condition='credit_good')
process.add_sequence_flow(credit_check_gateway, reject, condition='credit_bad')

process.add_sequence_flow(process_docs, parallel_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(verify_income, parallel_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_gateway, join_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, approve)
process.add_sequence_flow(approve, end)

# Bad credit path
process.add_sequence_flow(reject, end)

approve = Task(id='approve', name='Approve', task_type='user')
reject = Task(id='reject', name='Reject', task_type='user')

process.add_sequence_flow(custom_gateway, reject, condition='path2')

process.add_sequence_flow(reject, end)

parts.text: I will create a BPMN process with a start event, a "collect data" user task, and a gateway named "custom decision gateway" with an invalid `gateway_type` of 'conditional'. From this gateway, two paths will diverge: "path1" leading to an "approve" task and then to an end event, and "path2" leading to a "reject" task and then to an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process and use `Task` for user tasks and `Gateway` with `gateway_type='conditional'` for the invalid gateway. Finally, I will use the `validate_process` tool to check the validity of the generated BPMN code.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, Gateway, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='invalid_gateway_type_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
collect_data = UserTask(id='collect_data', name='Collect Data')

custom_gateway = Gateway(id='custom_gateway', name='Custom Decision Gateway', gateway_type='conditional')

approve = Task(id='approve', name='Approve', task_type='user')
reject = Task(id='reject', name='Reject', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(collect_data)
process.add_element(custom_gateway)
process.add_element(approve)
process.add_element(reject)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, collect_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(collect_data, custom_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(custom_gateway, approve, condition='path1')
process.add_sequence_flow(custom_gateway, reject, condition='path2')

process.add_sequence_flow(approve, end)
process.add_sequence_flow(reject, end)

archive_invoice = UserTask(id='archive_invoice_task', name='Archive Invoice')
end = EndEvent(id='end_event')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_invoice)

process.add_element(archive_invoice)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_invoice)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_invoice, validate_invoice)
process.add_sequence_flow(validate_invoice, process_payment)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_payment, archive_invoice)
process.add_sequence_flow(archive_invoice, end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, EndEvent

task_b = Task(id='task1', name='Task B', task_type='user')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='loop_back_to_start_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
process_task = Task(id='process_task', name='Process Task', task_type='user')
decision_gateway = Gateway(id='decision_gateway', name='Decision Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(process_task)
process.add_element(decision_gateway)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, process_task)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_task, decision_gateway)

# Invalid loop back to start
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, start, condition='retry')

# Path to end
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, end, condition='done')

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, IntermediateMessageCatchEvent, EndEvent

process = Process(name='support_ticket_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
receive_ticket = UserTask(id='receive_ticket', name='Receive Ticket')
wait_for_response = IntermediateMessageCatchEvent(id='wait_for_response', name='Wait for Customer Response')
process_response = UserTask(id='process_response', name='Process Response')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_ticket)
process.add_element(wait_for_response)
process.add_element(process_response)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_ticket)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_ticket, wait_for_response)
process.add_sequence_flow(wait_for_response, process_response)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_response, end)

parts.text: I will create a support ticket process with a start event, a user task "receive ticket", an intermediate message catch event "wait for customer response", a user task "process response", and an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used. I will use `Task` with `task_type='user'` for the user tasks and `IntermediateMessageCatchEvent` for the message catch event. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, IntermediateMessageCatchEvent, EndEvent

process = Process(name='support_ticket_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
receive_ticket = UserTask(id='receive_ticket', name='Receive Ticket')
wait_for_response = IntermediateMessageCatchEvent(id='wait_for_response', name='Wait for Customer Response')
process_response = UserTask(id='process_response', name='Process Response')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_ticket)
process.add_element(wait_for_response)
process.add_element(process_response)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_ticket)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_ticket, wait_for_response)
process.add_sequence_flow(wait_for_response, process_response)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_response, end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, IntermediateThrowEvent, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='notification_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
prepare_notification = UserTask(id='prepare_notification', name='Prepare Notification')

send_notification = IntermediateThrowEvent(id='send_notification_signal', name='Send Notification Signal', event_type='signal')

log_completion = Task(id='log_completion', name='Log Completion', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(prepare_notification)
process.add_element(send_notification)
process.add_element(log_completion)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, prepare_notification)
process.add_sequence_flow(prepare_notification, send_notification)
process.add_sequence_flow(send_notification, log_completion)
process.add_sequence_flow(log_completion, end)

parts.text: I will create a notification workflow with a start event, a user task "prepare notification", an intermediate throw signal event "send notification signal", a service task "log completion", and an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used. I will use `Task` with `task_type='user'` for the user task and `task_type='service'` for the service task, and `IntermediateThrowEvent` with `event_type='signal'` for the signal event. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, IntermediateThrowEvent, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='notification_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
prepare_notification = UserTask(id='prepare_notification', name='Prepare Notification')

send_notification = IntermediateThrowEvent(id='send_notification_signal', name='Send Notification Signal', event_type='signal')

log_completion = Task(id='log_completion', name='Log Completion', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(prepare_notification)
process.add_element(send_notification)
process.add_element(log_completion)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, prepare_notification)
process.add_sequence_flow(prepare_notification, send_notification)
process.add_sequence_flow(send_notification, log_completion)
process.add_sequence_flow(log_completion, end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='data_pipeline_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
collect_data = Task(id='collect_data', name='Collect Data', task_type='service')
transform_data = Task(id='transform_data', name='Transform Data', task_type='service')
validate_data = Task(id='validate_data', name='Validate Data', task_type='user')
store_data = Task(id='store_data', name='Store Data', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(collect_data)
process.add_element(transform_data)
process.add_element(validate_data)
process.add_element(store_data)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, collect_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(collect_data, transform_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(transform_data, validate_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(validate_data, store_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(store_data, end)

parts.text: I will create a data pipeline process with a start event, followed by four sequential tasks: "collect data" (service task), "transform data" (service task), "validate data" (user task), and "store data" (service task). The process will end with an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='data_pipeline_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
collect_data = Task(id='collect_data', name='Collect Data', task_type='service')
transform_data = Task(id='transform_data', name='Transform Data', task_type='service')
validate_data = Task(id='validate_data', name='Validate Data', task_type='user')
store_data = Task(id='store_data', name='Store Data', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(collect_data)
process.add_element(transform_data)
process.add_element(validate_data)
process.add_element(store_data)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, collect_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(collect_data, transform_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(transform_data, validate_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(validate_data, store_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(store_data, end)

process = Process(name='balanced_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
gather_requirements = UserTask(id='gather_requirements', name='Gather Requirements')
process_data = Task(id='process_data', name='Process Data', task_type='service')
review_results = UserTask(id='review_results', name='Review Results')
generate_report = Task(id='generate_report', name='Generate Report', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(gather_requirements)
process.add_element(process_data)
process.add_element(review_results)
process.add_element(generate_report)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, gather_requirements)
process.add_sequence_flow(gather_requirements, process_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_data, review_results)
process.add_sequence_flow(review_results, generate_report)
process.add_sequence_flow(generate_report, end)

parts.text: I will create a balanced workflow with a start event, followed by four sequential tasks: "gather requirements" (user task), "process data" (service task), "review results" (user task), and "generate report" (service task). The process will end with an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used. I will use `Task` with `task_type='user'` and `task_type='service'` as appropriate. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='balanced_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
gather_requirements = UserTask(id='gather_requirements', name='Gather Requirements')
process_data = Task(id='process_data', name='Process Data', task_type='service')
review_results = UserTask(id='review_results', name='Review Results')
generate_report = Task(id='generate_report', name='Generate Report', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(gather_requirements)
process.add_element(process_data)
process.add_element(review_results)
process.add_element(generate_report)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, gather_requirements)
process.add_sequence_flow(gather_requirements, process_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_data, review_results)
process.add_sequence_flow(review_results, generate_report)
process.add_sequence_flow(generate_report, end)

parts.text: The import error for `UserTask` persists. I will replace `UserTask` with `Task` and specify `task_type='user'` for the "gather requirements" and "review results" tasks. I will also ensure that "process data" and "generate report" are of `task_type='service'`. The `add_element` and `add_sequence_flow` methods will be used to define the process.

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ScriptTask, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='script_task_no_gateway_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
prepare_data = UserTask(id='prepare_data', name='Prepare Data')
run_script = ScriptTask(id='run_script', name='Run Transformation Script', script='print("Running script...")')
save_results = Task(id='save_results', name='Save Results', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(prepare_data)
process.add_element(run_script)
process.add_element(save_results)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, prepare_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(prepare_data, run_script)
process.add_sequence_flow(run_script, save_results)
process.add_sequence_flow(save_results, end)

gateway = Gateway(id='gateway', name='Split/Join Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

process.add_sequence_flow(start, task2)

parts.text: I will create a BPMN process that starts with two tasks, "task1" and "task2". Both of these tasks will flow into a single gateway. This gateway will then have two outgoing flows: one to "task3" and another to "task4". This configuration is intended to violate the rule that requires separate split and join gateways. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='split_join_gateway_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
task1 = Task(id='task1', name='Task 1', task_type='user')
task2 = Task(id='task2', name='Task 2', task_type='user')

gateway = Gateway(id='gateway', name='Split/Join Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

task3 = Task(id='task3', name='Task 3', task_type='service')
task4 = Task(id='task4', name='Task 4', task_type='service')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(task1)
process.add_element(task2)
process.add_element(gateway)
process.add_element(task3)
process.add_element(task4)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, task1)
process.add_sequence_flow(start, task2)

process.add_sequence_flow(task1, gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(task2, gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(gateway, task3)
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway, task4)

process.add_sequence_flow(task3, end)
process.add_sequence_flow(task4, end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ExclusiveGateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='retry_mechanism_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
attempt_task = UserTask(id='attempt_task', name='Attempt Task')

decision_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='decision_gateway', name='Check Result', gateway_type='exclusive')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(attempt_task)
process.add_element(decision_gateway)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, attempt_task)
process.add_sequence_flow(attempt_task, decision_gateway)

# If success
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, end, condition='success')

# If failure (loop back to attempt_task)
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, attempt_task, condition='failure')

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='empty_loop_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
task1 = Task(id='task1', name='Task 1', task_type='user')

gateway1 = Gateway(id='gateway1', name='Gateway 1', gateway_type='exclusive')
gateway2 = Gateway(id='gateway2', name='Gateway 2', gateway_type='exclusive')

task2 = Task(id='task2', name='Task 2', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(task1)
process.add_element(gateway1)
process.add_element(gateway2)
process.add_element(task2)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, task1)
process.add_sequence_flow(task1, gateway1)

# Loop
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway1, gateway2, condition='loop')
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway2, gateway1, condition='continue_loop')

# Other path
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway1, task2, condition='done')
process.add_sequence_flow(task2, end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ExclusiveGateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='infinite_loop_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')

parts.text: I will create a BPMN process with a start event, a "process task" user task, and an exclusive gateway. The gateway will have a conditional outgoing flow back to the "process task", creating an infinite loop with no exit path to an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ExclusiveGateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='infinite_loop_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
process_task = UserTask(id='process_task', name='Process Task')

decision_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='decision_gateway', name='Decision Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

# No end event is reachable as per the requirement

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(process_task)
process.add_element(decision_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, process_task)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_task, decision_gateway)

# Loop back to process_task with no exit path
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, process_task, condition='retry')

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, ExclusiveGateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='symmetric_branching_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
split_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='split_gateway', name='Split Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

approve_task = Task(id='approve_task', name='Approve Task', task_type='user')
reject_task = Task(id='reject_task', name='Reject Task', task_type='user')

join_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

notify = Task(id='notify', name='Notify', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(split_gateway)
process.add_element(approve_task)
process.add_element(reject_task)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(notify)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, split_gateway)

# Split paths
process.add_sequence_flow(split_gateway, approve_task, condition='path1')
process.add_sequence_flow(split_gateway, reject_task, condition='path2')

# Join paths
process.add_sequence_flow(approve_task, join_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(reject_task, join_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, notify)
process.add_sequence_flow(notify, end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, ExclusiveGateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='symmetric_branching_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
split_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='split_gateway', name='Split Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

approve_task = Task(id='approve_task', name='Approve Task', task_type='user')
reject_task = Task(id='reject_task', name='Reject Task', task_type='user')

join_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

notify = Task(id='notify', name='Notify', task_type='service')
end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(split_gateway)
process.add_element(approve_task)
process.add_element(reject_task)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(notify)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, split_gateway)

# Split paths
process.add_sequence_flow(split_gateway, approve_task, condition='path1')
process.add_sequence_flow(split_gateway, reject_task, condition='path2')

# Join paths
process.add_sequence_flow(approve_task, join_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(reject_task, join_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, notify)
process.add_sequence_flow(notify, end)

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='comprehensive_order_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
receive_order = Task(id='receive_order', name='Receive Order', task_type='user')

parallel_split = Gateway(id='parallel_split', name='Parallel Split', gateway_type='parallel')

process_payment = Task(id='process_payment', name='Process Payment', task_type='service')
prepare_shipment = Task(id='prepare_shipment', name='Prepare Shipment', task_type='user')

join_gateway = Gateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')

quality_check_gateway = Gateway(id='quality_check_gateway', name='Quality Check', gateway_type='exclusive')

ship = Task(id='ship', name='Ship Order', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_order)
process.add_element(parallel_split)
process.add_element(process_payment)
process.add_element(prepare_shipment)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(quality_check_gateway)
process.add_element(ship)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_order, parallel_split)

# Parallel paths
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, process_payment)
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, prepare_shipment)

# Join paths
process.add_sequence_flow(process_payment, join_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(prepare_shipment, join_gateway)

# After join
process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, quality_check_gateway)

# Quality check branching
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, ship, condition='pass')
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, prepare_shipment, condition='fail') # Loop back to prepare shipment

process.add_sequence_flow(ship, end)

parts.text: I will create a comprehensive order workflow starting with a start event, followed by "receive order" user task. Then, a parallel gateway will split the process into two parallel paths: "process payment" service task and "prepare shipment" user task. These paths will converge at a join gateway. From there, an exclusive gateway "quality check gateway" will branch based on the result. If the quality check passes, it will proceed to "ship" (user task) and then to the end event. If it fails, it will loop back to "prepare shipment" user task. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` for this, employing `Task` for user and service tasks, and `Gateway` for exclusive and parallel gateways. Finally, I will validate the process using `validate_process`.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='comprehensive_order_workflow')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
receive_order = Task(id='receive_order', name='Receive Order', task_type='user')

parallel_split = Gateway(id='parallel_split', name='Parallel Split', gateway_type='parallel')

process_payment = Task(id='process_payment', name='Process Payment', task_type='service')
prepare_shipment = Task(id='prepare_shipment', name='Prepare Shipment', task_type='user')

join_gateway = Gateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')

quality_check_gateway = Gateway(id='quality_check_gateway', name='Quality Check', gateway_type='exclusive')

ship = Task(id='ship', name='Ship Order', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_order)
process.add_element(parallel_split)
process.add_element(process_payment)
process.add_element(prepare_shipment)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(quality_check_gateway)
process.add_element(ship)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_order, parallel_split)

# Parallel paths
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, process_payment)
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, prepare_shipment)

# Join paths
process.add_sequence_flow(process_payment, join_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(prepare_shipment, join_gateway)

# After join
process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, quality_check_gateway)

# Quality check branching
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, ship, condition='pass')
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, prepare_shipment, condition='fail') # Loop back to prepare shipment

process.add_sequence_flow(ship, end)

process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(task1, end)

parts.text: I will create a BPMN process with a start event, "task1", and an end event. Additionally, I will include "task2" and "task3" which will not be connected to the start event or any other reachable part of the process, thus making them unreachable. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used. Finally, I will validate the process using the `validate_process` tool.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, EndEvent

process = Process(name='isolated_task_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
task1 = Task(id='task1', name='Task 1', task_type='user')

# Unreachable tasks
task2 = Task(id='task2', name='Task 2', task_type='service')
task3 = Task(id='task3', name='Task 3', task_type='service')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(task1)
process.add_element(task2)
process.add_element(task3)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, task1)
process.add_sequence_flow(task1, end)

results.generated_code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='comprehensive_order_fulfillment')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
validate_order = Task(id='validate_order', name='Validate Order', task_type='user')

parallel_split = Gateway(id='parallel_split', name='Parallel Split', gateway_type='parallel')

check_inventory = Task(id='check_inventory', name='Check Inventory', task_type='service')
process_payment = Task(id='process_payment', name='Process Payment', task_type='service')

join_gateway = Gateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')

quality_check_gateway = Gateway(id='quality_check_gateway', name='Checks Results', gateway_type='exclusive')

ship = Task(id='ship', name='Ship Order', task_type='user')
cancel_order = Task(id='cancel_order', name='Cancel Order', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(validate_order)
process.add_element(parallel_split)
process.add_element(check_inventory)
process.add_element(process_payment)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(quality_check_gateway)
process.add_element(ship)
process.add_element(cancel_order)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, validate_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(validate_order, parallel_split)

# Parallel paths
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, check_inventory)
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, process_payment)

# Join paths
process.add_sequence_flow(check_inventory, join_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_payment, join_gateway)

# After join
process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, quality_check_gateway)

# Quality check branching
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, ship, condition='success')
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, cancel_order, condition='failure')

process.add_sequence_flow(ship, end)
process.add_sequence_flow(cancel_order, end)

parts.text: I will create a comprehensive order fulfillment workflow. It will begin with a start event, followed by a "validate order" user task. Then, a parallel gateway will split the process into two concurrent paths: "check inventory" (service task) and "process payment" (service task). These paths will then join at a parallel gateway. After joining, an exclusive gateway "checks results" will determine the next step. If the results are successful, it will proceed to a "ship" user task, then to a "service" task, and finally to an end event. If the quality check fails, it will go to a "cancel order" user task and then to an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` for this, employing `Task` for user and service tasks, and `Gateway` for parallel and exclusive gateways. Finally, I will validate the process using `validate_process`.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='comprehensive_order_fulfillment')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
validate_order = Task(id='validate_order', name='Validate Order', task_type='user')

parallel_split = Gateway(id='parallel_split', name='Parallel Split', gateway_type='parallel')

check_inventory = Task(id='check_inventory', name='Check Inventory', task_type='service')
process_payment = Task(id='process_payment', name='Process Payment', task_type='service')

join_gateway = Gateway(id='join_gateway', name='Join Gateway', gateway_type='parallel')

quality_check_gateway = Gateway(id='quality_check_gateway', name='Checks Results', gateway_type='exclusive')

ship = Task(id='ship', name='Ship Order', task_type='user')
cancel_order = Task(id='cancel_order', name='Cancel Order', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(validate_order)
process.add_element(parallel_split)
process.add_element(check_inventory)
process.add_element(process_payment)
process.add_element(join_gateway)
process.add_element(quality_check_gateway)
process.add_element(ship)
process.add_element(cancel_order)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, validate_order)
process.add_sequence_flow(validate_order, parallel_split)

# Parallel paths
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, check_inventory)
process.add_sequence_flow(parallel_split, process_payment)

# Join paths
process.add_sequence_flow(check_inventory, join_gateway)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_payment, join_gateway)

# After join
process.add_sequence_flow(join_gateway, quality_check_gateway)

# Quality check branching
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, ship, condition='success')
process.add_sequence_flow(quality_check_gateway, cancel_order, condition='failure')

process.add_sequence_flow(ship, end)
process.add_sequence_flow(cancel_order, end)

results.generated_code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, Task, ExclusiveGateway, IntermediateMessageCatchEvent, EndEvent

process = Process(name='loan_approval_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
submit_application = UserTask(id='submit_application', name='Submit Application')
check_credit = Task(id='check_credit', name='Check Credit', task_type='service')

decision_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='decision_gateway', name='Decision Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

generate_offer = Task(id='generate_offer', name='Generate Offer', task_type='service')

wait_for_docs = IntermediateMessageCatchEvent(id='wait_for_docs', name='Wait for Docs')
re_evaluate = UserTask(id='re_evaluate', name='Re-evaluate')

notify_rejection = Task(id='notify_rejection', name='Notify Rejection', task_type='service')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(submit_application)
process.add_element(check_credit)
process.add_element(decision_gateway)
process.add_element(generate_offer)
process.add_element(wait_for_docs)
process.add_element(re_evaluate)
process.add_element(notify_rejection)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, submit_application)
process.add_sequence_flow(submit_application, check_credit)
process.add_sequence_flow(check_credit, decision_gateway)

# If approved
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, generate_offer, condition='approved')
process.add_sequence_flow(generate_offer, end)

# If review needed
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, wait_for_docs, condition='review_needed')
process.add_sequence_flow(wait_for_docs, re_evaluate)
process.add_sequence_flow(re_evaluate, decision_gateway)

# If rejected
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, notify_rejection, condition='rejected')
process.add_sequence_flow(notify_rejection, end)

parts.text: I will create a loan approval process with a start event, followed by a "submit application" user task. Then, a "check credit" service task will precede an exclusive gateway for decision making. If approved, it leads to a "generate offer" service task and then to an end event. If review is needed, it goes to a "wait for docs" intermediate message catch event, then to a "re-evaluate" user task, which loops back to the decision gateway. If rejected, it goes to a "notify rejection" service task and then to an end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` for this, employing `Task` for user and service tasks, `Gateway` for the exclusive gateway, and `IntermediateMessageCatchEvent` for the wait event. Finally, I will validate the process using `validate_process`.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, Task, ExclusiveGateway, IntermediateMessageCatchEvent, EndEvent

process = Process(name='loan_approval_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
submit_application = UserTask(id='submit_application', name='Submit Application')
check_credit = Task(id='check_credit', name='Check Credit', task_type='service')

decision_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='decision_gateway', name='Decision Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

generate_offer = Task(id='generate_offer', name='Generate Offer', task_type='service')

wait_for_docs = IntermediateMessageCatchEvent(id='wait_for_docs', name='Wait for Docs')
re_evaluate = UserTask(id='re_evaluate', name='Re-evaluate')

notify_rejection = Task(id='notify_rejection', name='Notify Rejection', task_type='service')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(submit_application)
process.add_element(check_credit)
process.add_element(decision_gateway)
process.add_element(generate_offer)
process.add_element(wait_for_docs)
process.add_element(re_evaluate)
process.add_element(notify_rejection)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, submit_application)
process.add_sequence_flow(submit_application, check_credit)
process.add_sequence_flow(check_credit, decision_gateway)

# If approved
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, generate_offer, condition='approved')
process.add_sequence_flow(generate_offer, end)

# If review needed
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, wait_for_docs, condition='review_needed')
process.add_sequence_flow(wait_for_docs, re_evaluate)
process.add_sequence_flow(re_evaluate, decision_gateway)

# If rejected
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, notify_rejection, condition='rejected')
process.add_sequence_flow(notify_rejection, end)

parts.text: The import error for `UserTask` persists. I will replace `UserTask` with `Task` and specify `task_type='user'` for "submit application", "re-evaluate", and "notify rejection" tasks. I will also replace `ExclusiveGateway` with `Gateway` and specify `gateway_type='exclusive'`, and `IntermediateMessageCatchEvent` with `IntermediateCatchEvent` and specify `event_type='message'`. I will ensure all other elements and sequence flows are correctly defined. The `add_element` and `add_sequence_flow` methods will be used to define the process.

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, IntermediateCatchEvent, EndEvent

process = Process(name='loan_approval_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
submit_application = Task(id='submit_application', name='Submit Application', task_type='user')
check_credit = Task(id='check_credit', name='Check Credit', task_type='service')

decision_gateway = Gateway(id='decision_gateway', name='Decision Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

generate_offer = Task(id='generate_offer', name='Generate Offer', task_type='service')

wait_for_docs = IntermediateCatchEvent(id='wait_for_docs', name='Wait for Docs', event_type='message')
re_evaluate = Task(id='re_evaluate', name='Re-evaluate', task_type='user')

notify_rejection = Task(id='notify_rejection', name='Notify Rejection', task_type='user')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(submit_application)
process.add_element(check_credit)
process.add_element(decision_gateway)
process.add_element(generate_offer)
process.add_element(wait_for_docs)
process.add_element(re_evaluate)
process.add_element(notify_rejection)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, submit_application)
process.add_sequence_flow(submit_application, check_credit)
process.add_sequence_flow(check_credit, decision_gateway)

# If approved
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, generate_offer, condition='approved')
process.add_sequence_flow(generate_offer, end)

# If review needed
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, wait_for_docs, condition='review_needed')
process.add_sequence_flow(wait_for_docs, re_evaluate)
process.add_sequence_flow(re_evaluate, decision_gateway)

# If rejected
process.add_sequence_flow(decision_gateway, notify_rejection, condition='rejected')
process.add_sequence_flow(notify_rejection, end)

parts.text: I will create a complex and deliberately invalid BPMN process. It will start with a start event, followed by five sequential tasks: "task1" (service), "task2" (service), "task3" (user), "task4" (service), and "task5" (service). This sequence violates the limit of four consecutive tasks. Then, a splitting gateway will branch into two paths: Path 1 leading to "end1", and Path 2 leading to "end2". This also violates the rule that paths must converge at a joining gateway. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used, and then validate it using `validate_process` to highlight these multiple rule violations.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='invalid_complex_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
task1 = Task(id='task1', name='Task 1', task_type='service')
task2 = Task(id='task2', name='Task 2', task_type='service')
task3 = Task(id='task3', name='Task 3', task_type='user')
task4 = Task(id='task4', name='Task 4', task_type='service')
task5 = Task(id='task5', name='Task 5', task_type='service')

split_gateway = Gateway(id='split_gateway', name='Split Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

end1 = EndEvent(id='end1')
end2 = EndEvent(id='end2')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(task1)
process.add_element(task2)
process.add_element(task3)
process.add_element(task4)
process.add_element(task5)
process.add_element(split_gateway)
process.add_element(end1)
process.add_element(end2)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, task1)
process.add_sequence_flow(task1, task2)
process.add_sequence_flow(task2, task3)
process.add_sequence_flow(task3, task4)
process.add_sequence_flow(task4, task5)
process.add_sequence_flow(task5, split_gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(split_gateway, end1, condition='path1')
process.add_sequence_flow(split_gateway, end2, condition='path2')

        Task(id='task1', name='Task 1', task_type='service'),

        Task(id='task5', name='Task 5', task_type='service'),

        ('split_gateway', 'end2', 'path2')
    ]
)

results.generated_code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ExclusiveGateway, Task, IntermediateTimerEvent, EndEvent

process = Process(name='customer_support_escalation')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
receive_ticket = UserTask(id='receive_ticket', name='Receive Ticket')

priority_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='priority_gateway', name='Priority Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

# High priority path
immediate_response = UserTask(id='immediate_response', name='Immediate Response')

# Normal priority path
wait_for_assignment = IntermediateTimerEvent(id='wait_for_assignment', name='Wait for Assignment', event_type='timer')
assign_agent = Task(id='assign_agent', name='Assign Agent', task_type='service')
process_response = UserTask(id='process_response', name='Process Response')

satisfaction_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='satisfaction_gateway', name='Satisfaction Check', gateway_type='exclusive')

close_ticket = Task(id='close_ticket', name='Close Ticket', task_type='service')

escalate = UserTask(id='escalate', name='Escalate')
manager_review = UserTask(id='manager_review', name='Manager Review')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_ticket)
process.add_element(priority_gateway)
process.add_element(immediate_response)
process.add_element(wait_for_assignment)
process.add_element(assign_agent)
process.add_element(process_response)
process.add_element(satisfaction_gateway)
process.add_element(close_ticket)
process.add_element(escalate)
process.add_element(manager_review)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_ticket)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_ticket, priority_gateway)

# High priority path
process.add_sequence_flow(priority_gateway, immediate_response, condition='high')
process.add_sequence_flow(immediate_response, end)

# Normal priority path
process.add_sequence_flow(priority_gateway, wait_for_assignment, condition='normal')
process.add_sequence_flow(wait_for_assignment, assign_agent)
process.add_sequence_flow(assign_agent, process_response)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_response, satisfaction_gateway)

# Satisfaction check branching
process.add_sequence_flow(satisfaction_gateway, close_ticket, condition='satisfied')
process.add_sequence_flow(satisfaction_gateway, escalate, condition='not_satisfied')

process.add_sequence_flow(close_ticket, end)
process.add_sequence_flow(escalate, manager_review)
process.add_sequence_flow(manager_review, end)
results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements in Python.
    - You need to always import using `from bpmn_lang import Process, ...`
    - Call the validate_process tool to check the validity of your BPMN code and learn from the errors description provided by the tool.
    - Dont try multiple times the same thing, if the error is the same, just fix it.
    

Create a customer support escalation: start → receive ticket (user) → priority gateway (exclusive) → high priority: immediate response (user) → end; normal priority: wait for assignment (intermediate timer) → assign agent (service) → process (user) → satisfaction check gateway → if satisfied: close → end; if not: escalate (user) → manager review (user) → end
results.final_response:
I will create a customer support escalation process. It starts with a "receive ticket" user task. Then, an exclusive gateway "priority gateway" will determine the path. For high priority, it goes to "immediate response" user task and then to the end event. For normal priority, it proceeds to a "wait for assignment" intermediate timer event, then to "assign agent" service task, followed by "process" user task. After that, a "satisfaction check gateway" will decide whether to go to "close" (which I'll represent as a service task for simplicity) and then to the end event, or if not satisfied, to "escalate" user task, then "manager review" user task, and finally to the end event. parts.text: I will create a customer support escalation process. It starts with a "receive ticket" user task. Then, an exclusive gateway "priority gateway" will determine the path. For high priority, it goes to "immediate response" user task and then to the end event. For normal priority, it proceeds to a "wait for assignment" intermediate timer event, then to "assign agent" service task, followed by "process" user task. After that, a "satisfaction check gateway" will decide whether to go to "close" (which I'll represent as a service task for simplicity) and then to the end event, or if not satisfied, to "escalate" user task, then "manager review" user task, and finally to the end event. I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring correct element types and sequence flows, including conditional paths and the loop-back/escalation logic. I will use `Task` with appropriate `task_type`, `Gateway` with `gateway_type='exclusive'`, and `IntermediateTimerEvent` with `event_type='timer'`. Finally, I will validate the process using `validate_process`.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, UserTask, ExclusiveGateway, Task, IntermediateTimerEvent, EndEvent

process = Process(name='customer_support_escalation')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
receive_ticket = UserTask(id='receive_ticket', name='Receive Ticket')

priority_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='priority_gateway', name='Priority Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

# High priority path
immediate_response = UserTask(id='immediate_response', name='Immediate Response')

# Normal priority path
wait_for_assignment = IntermediateTimerEvent(id='wait_for_assignment', name='Wait for Assignment', event_type='timer')
assign_agent = Task(id='assign_agent', name='Assign Agent', task_type='service')
process_response = UserTask(id='process_response', name='Process Response')

satisfaction_gateway = ExclusiveGateway(id='satisfaction_gateway', name='Satisfaction Check', gateway_type='exclusive')

close_ticket = Task(id='close_ticket', name='Close Ticket', task_type='service')

escalate = UserTask(id='escalate', name='Escalate')
manager_review = UserTask(id='manager_review', name='Manager Review')

end = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(receive_ticket)
process.add_element(priority_gateway)
process.add_element(immediate_response)
process.add_element(wait_for_assignment)
process.add_element(assign_agent)
process.add_element(process_response)
process.add_element(satisfaction_gateway)
process.add_element(close_ticket)
process.add_element(escalate)
process.add_element(manager_review)
process.add_element(end)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, receive_ticket)
process.add_sequence_flow(receive_ticket, priority_gateway)

# High priority path
process.add_sequence_flow(priority_gateway, immediate_response, condition='high')
process.add_sequence_flow(immediate_response, end)

# Normal priority path
process.add_sequence_flow(priority_gateway, wait_for_assignment, condition='normal')
process.add_sequence_flow(wait_for_assignment, assign_agent)
process.add_sequence_flow(assign_agent, process_response)
process.add_sequence_flow(process_response, satisfaction_gateway)

# Satisfaction check branching
process.add_sequence_flow(satisfaction_gateway, close_ticket, condition='satisfied')
process.add_sequence_flow(satisfaction_gateway, escalate, condition='not_satisfied')

process.add_sequence_flow(close_ticket, end)
process.add_sequence_flow(escalate, manager_review)
process.add_sequence_flow(manager_review, end)

process = Process(name='customer_support_escalation', 
    elements=[
        StartEvent(id='start'),
        Task(id='receive_ticket', name='Receive Ticket', task_type='user'),
        Gateway(id='priority_gateway', name='Priority Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive'),
        Task(id='immediate_response', name='Immediate Response', task_type='user'),
        IntermediateCatchEvent(id='wait_for_assignment', name='Wait for Assignment', event_type='timer'),
        Task(id='assign_agent', name='Assign Agent', task_type='service'),
        Task(id='process_response', name='Process Response', task_type='user'),
        Gateway(id='satisfaction_gateway', name='Satisfaction Check', gateway_type='exclusive'),
        Task(id='close_ticket', name='Close Ticket', task_type='service'),
        Task(id='escalate', name='Escalate', task_type='user'),
        Task(id='manager_review', name='Manager Review', task_type='user'),
        EndEvent(id='end')
    ],
    sequence_flow=[
        ('start', 'receive_ticket'),
        ('receive_ticket', 'priority_gateway'),
        ('priority_gateway', 'immediate_response', 'high'),
        ('priority_gateway', 'wait_for_assignment', 'normal'),
        ('immediate_response', 'end'),
        ('wait_for_assignment', 'assign_agent'),
        ('assign_agent', 'process_response'),
        ('process_response', 'satisfaction_gateway'),
        ('satisfaction_gateway', 'close_ticket', 'satisfied'),
        ('satisfaction_gateway', 'escalate', 'not_satisfied'),
        ('close_ticket', 'end'),
        ('escalate', 'manager_review'),
        ('manager_review', 'end')
    ]
)

results.generated_code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, ScriptTask, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='broken_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
prepare_data = Task(id='prepare_data', name='Prepare Data', task_type='user')

# Script task without error handling
run_script = ScriptTask(id='run_script', name='Run Script', script='print("Executing script...")')

# Gateway with multiple in/out (implicitly represented by multiple outgoing flows from one gateway)
gateway = Gateway(id='gateway', name='Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

# Path 1: Loop back to start (invalid)
# Task 3 (which would be part of the loop) is omitted for simplicity to focus on the loop itself back to start.

# Path 2: Dead-end task
dead_end_task = Task(id='dead_end_task', name='Dead End Task', task_type='service')

# End event for the valid path
end_event = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(prepare_data)
process.add_element(run_script)
process.add_element(gateway)
process.add_element(dead_end_task)
process.add_element(end_event)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, prepare_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(prepare_data, run_script)
process.add_sequence_flow(run_script, gateway)

# Path 1: Invalid loop back to start
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway, start, condition='loop_back')

# Path 2: Dead-end task
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway, dead_end_task, condition='continue')

# No outgoing flow from dead_end_task
results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements in Python.
    - You need to always import using `from bpmn_lang import Process, ...`
    - Call the validate_process tool to check the validity of your BPMN code and learn from the errors description provided by the tool.
    - Dont try multiple times the same thing, if the error is the same, just fix it.
     IMPORTANT: You must create a process with MIXED violations: loop to start event, script without error handling, dead end, and improper gateway.

Create a deliberately broken process: start → prepare (user) → transform (script task, no error handling) → gateway (multiple in, multiple out) → path1: loops back to start (invalid); path2: dead-end task with no path to end

parts.text:
I will create a BPMN process that incorporates multiple violations:
1.  **Loop back to start event:** The process will attempt to loop back to the start event, which is an invalid BPMN construct.
2.  **Script task without error handling:** A script task will be included without any error handling mechanisms.
3.  **Dead-end task:** A task will be present that is not reachable from the start event and has no path to an end event.
4.  **Improper gateway:** A gateway will be used with multiple incoming and multiple outgoing flows, violating proper gateway usage.

The process flow will be: start → prepare (user task) → transform (script task) → gateway → path1 loops back to start; path2 leads to a dead-end task.

I will use `bpmn_lang.py` to define this process, ensuring the correct elements and sequence flows are used to demonstrate these violations. Finally, I will call the `validate_process` tool to identify these errors.

args.code:
from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, ScriptTask, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='broken_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
prepare_data = Task(id='prepare_data', name='Prepare Data', task_type='user')

# Script task without error handling
run_script = ScriptTask(id='run_script', name='Run Script', script='print("Executing script...")')

# Gateway with multiple in/out (implicitly represented by multiple outgoing flows from one gateway)
gateway = Gateway(id='gateway', name='Gateway', gateway_type='exclusive')

# Path 1: Loop back to start (invalid)
# Task 3 (which would be part of the loop) is omitted for simplicity to focus on the loop itself back to start.

# Path 2: Dead-end task
dead_end_task = Task(id='dead_end_task', name='Dead End Task', task_type='service')

# End event for the valid path
end_event = EndEvent(id='end')

process.add_element(start)
process.add_element(prepare_data)
process.add_element(run_script)
process.add_element(gateway)
process.add_element(dead_end_task)
process.add_element(end_event)

process.add_sequence_flow(start, prepare_data)
process.add_sequence_flow(prepare_data, run_script)
process.add_sequence_flow(run_script, gateway)

# Path 1: Invalid loop back to start
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway, start, condition='loop_back')

# Path 2: Dead-end task
process.add_sequence_flow(gateway, dead_end_task, condition='continue')

# No outgoing flow from dead_end_task

from bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, Task, Gateway, EndEvent

process = Process(name='broken_process')

start = StartEvent(id='start')
prepare_data = Task(id='prepare_data', name='Prepare Data', task_type='user')

# Gateway with multiple in/out (implicitly represented by multiple outgoing flows from one gateway)

process.add_sequence_flow(run_script, decision_gateway)
